name: Sagamok Anishnawbek
notes: Ojibwe community on Sagamok 46 reserve along the Spanish River, approximately 120 km west of Sudbury. Member of the Anishinabek Nation. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Serpent River First Nation
notes: Anishinaabe community on Serpent River 7 reserve near Cutler, Ontario. Member of the North Shore Tribal Council. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Mississauga First Nation
notes: Ojibwe community on Mississagi River 8 reserve, located directly west of Blind River on the Mississagi River. Member of the North Shore Tribal Council. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Thessalon First Nation
notes: Ojibwe community on Thessalon 12 reserve near Thessalon, Ontario. Member of the Anishinabek Nation. Treaty: Robinson Huron and Pennefather.

name: Garden River First Nation
notes: Ojibwe community on Garden River 14 reserve, located east of Sault Ste. Marie along the Garden River. Also known as Ketegaunseebee. Member of the Association of Iroquois and Allied Indians. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Batchewana First Nation
notes: Ojibwe community with main reserve at Rankin Location 15D near Sault Ste. Marie. Also has Goulais Bay 15A and Obadjiwan 15E reserves. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Whitefish River First Nation
notes: Anishinaabe community on Whitefish River (Part) 4 reserve at Birch Island, on the north shore of Georgian Bay near the La Cloche Mountains. Member of the United Chiefs and Councils of Mnidoo Mnising. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Atikameksheng Anishnawbek
notes: Ojibwe community on Whitefish Lake 6 reserve near Naughton, southwest of Greater Sudbury. Formerly known as Whitefish Lake First Nation. Member of the Anishinabek Nation. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Wahnapitae First Nation
notes: Ojibwe community on Wahnapitei 11 reserve near Capreol, northeast of Greater Sudbury on Lake Wahnapitei. Member of the Anishinabek Nation. Treaty: Robinson Huron 1850.

name: Massey
notes: Settlement within the Township of Sables-Spanish Rivers (township population 3,237). Located at the junction of Highway 17 and Highway 553. Gateway to Chutes Provincial Park.

name: Espanola
notes: Largest town in the Sudbury District west region. Located on the Spanish River. Home to Espanola Regional Hospital and Health Centre. Service hub for surrounding First Nations communities.

name: Blind River
notes: Town on the North Channel of Lake Huron at the mouth of the Blind River. Service centre for Mississauga and Serpent River First Nations. Home to the North Shore Health Network.

name: Elliot Lake
notes: Former uranium mining city, now a retirement community. Located inland from the North Channel. Home to the Nuclear and Mining Museum.

name: Iron Bridge
notes: Settlement within the Municipality of Huron Shores on the Mississagi River. Located along Highway 17 between Thessalon and Blind River.

name: Spanish
notes: Small town at the mouth of the Spanish River on the North Channel of Lake Huron. Historic site of the Spanish Indian Residential School.

name: Sault Ste. Marie
notes: Largest city in the Algoma District. Located at the rapids of the St. Marys River connecting Lake Superior and Lake Huron. Major service centre for the entire North Shore region. Home to Algoma University and Sault College.

name: Greater Sudbury
notes: Largest city in Northern Ontario. Regional hub for health, education, and government services. Home to Laurentian University, Cambrian College, and Health Sciences North. Major service centre for surrounding First Nations.

name: Nairn Centre
notes: Small community in the Township of Nairn and Hyman, located along Highway 17 between Espanola and Sudbury. Includes the settlement of McKerrow.

name: Thessalon
notes: Town on the North Channel of Lake Huron at the mouth of the Thessalon River. Adjacent to Thessalon First Nation. Located along Highway 17.
